notes.anchor: out of all the Marcelli, and the Marcellini too
notes.note: The Marcelli were the senior branch of the patrician gens Claudia; the Marcellini (Cornelii Lentuli Marcellini) a related line. Cicero flatters the addressee that of this whole connection it is his own consulship (50 BC) that fell in the year of Cicero's victories.

notes.anchor: when my letter is read out, a decree of the Senate is made in the most honourable terms
notes.note: A victorious commander's dispatch (litterae laureatae) was read aloud in the Senate, which then debated honours; Cicero asks the consul to manage the reading and steer through a flattering senatus consultum.

notes.anchor: both in the decree of the supplicatio and in other matters
notes.note: Cicero presses C. Marcellus, now consul (50 BC), to secure the public thanksgiving voted for his Cilician campaign, the customary prelude to a hoped-for triumph.
